item.title: Explorer
item.description: Quartz features an explorer that allows you to navigate all files and folders on your site. It supports nested folders and is highly customizable.

item.title: Configuration
item.description: Quartz is meant to be extremely configurable, even if you don’t know any coding.

item.title: Hosting
item.description: Quartz effectively turns your Markdown files and other resources into a bundle of HTML, JS, and CSS files (a website!).

item.title: Latex
item.description: This plugin adds LaTeX support to Quartz. See Latex for more information.

item.title: Creating your own Quartz components
item.description: Warning This guide assumes you have experience writing JavaScript and are familiar with TypeScript.

item.title: Comments
item.description: Quartz also has the ability to hook into various providers to enable readers to leave comments on your site.

item.title: Private Pages
item.description: There may be some notes you want to avoid publishing as a website.

item.title: Higher-Order Layout Components
item.description: Quartz provides several higher-order components that help with layout composition and responsive design.

item.title: Quartz Showcase
item.description: Want to see what Quartz can do? Here are some cool community gardens: Quartz Documentation (this site!) Jacky Zhao’s Garden Aaron Pham’s Garden The Pond Eilleen’s Everything Notebook Morrowind Modding Wiki Stanford CME 302 Numerical Linear Algebra Socratica Toolbox A Pattern Language - Christopher A...

item.title: Welcome to Quartz 4
item.description: Quartz is a fast, batteries-included static-site generator that transforms Markdown content into fully functional websites.
